transcript.text: Oh, saw that Rapyd moved a bunch of content to reviewed.
not a bunch, but like three more.
Oh, yeah, they did.
Nice.
I left a note from Mark in one of them because he left notes and then I like resolved his comments and he left a note if he wanted to look at it again.
But, um, I don't know, look at this.
Aida Knezevic, Mark and Rapyd.
How's your Wednesday going?
It's good.
I'm doing, like, conversion panels on Tiro.
Oh, yeah, we're not done with that yet, yeah.
No, so I'm wrapping that up.
Hi, Mark.
How's it going?
Good.
Good.
Anybody else joining from your team?
I'm not sure if Nicolette's going to join or not.
Okay.
Good.
She can jump in.
Okay, sure.
Yeah, for today, I just wanted to go over the performance and then some content-related stuff.
But before we begin, I was wondering if you had anything you wanted to add to the agenda?
Because I noticed over the past couple of weeks, you do bring notes to meetings.
Yeah, I've got three things that I actually wanted to add or talk about today.
One is I wanted to request some blogs that we don't currently have on our list.
So I was going to see if I could do that.
And then I wanted to cover how we handle topics and subtopics within our blogs.
Okay.
Because that's part of our publication process.
And then I wanted to check up on the XML document and see if you had that ready because we're ready to take it.
And I apologize if there's...
Conversation about that, it's like that I missed, but that I wanted to cover.
And I think that is it for now.
Okay, great.
Yeah, cool.
Yeah, so XML stuff, it's ready to go.
Actually, let me show you what it looks like.
So we have the first batch started.
So this is just one article so far.
But I saw that you reviewed three additional articles.
So we're going to add them to this batch and send them to you, hopefully, by the end of the week.
So does this look okay?
I think this is what you're looking for, right?
I think so.
Why don't I, instead of adding it to it, why don't I just take this and send it to them?
Because you said it's just one article?
Yeah.
So that's not a bad place to start.
Okay.
So I'll take this, send it to my guys.
Is this in the black?
I just sent it in chat, but I can send it over in Slack as well.
It's fine.
Grab it out of the chat here.
All right, cool.
I'll use that.

And then, yeah, the other ones that we just finished here, I've got some blogs that Nicolette had reviewed that I went through and looked at today and then Mark has reviewed data, so those are ready for you.
Yeah, so I think what I'll do now is just...
Let's see, you change those from reviewed to when it's ready for Tom to put in there.
You change it to scheduled or what do you set it to when it's ready for Tom?
Scheduled.
Scheduled, okay.
Yeah.
Yeah, so those are ready for you and then I've got a couple more up here that I'll get through today.
Okay, great.
What kind of topics were you looking to suggest?
Oh, so...

You know, I didn't look through to see if these were already covered, but we are working on, Nicolette has been working on with me these blog posts that we've been making internally covering various different payment methods that Rapyd supports.
A lot of the payment methods that Rapyd supports are bank redirects, and we realize that they're all the same.

So instead of writing, like, do a bank redirect with Citibank or do a bank redirect with Bank of America, all you need is, like, one blog post on what's a bank redirect, and then I can kind of link any time that I list that, like, in the UK, Rapyd supports bank redirects with these five banks, I just link them all to that same article.
It's, like, it doesn't need a custom post.
So the first post I was going to do is, what is a bank redirect?
And then the second one, conversely, so when you do a bank redirect payment, it means I click on bank redirects as an option in, like,
Checkout and it actually gives me a selection of banks.

I select the bank and it redirects me to my bank to finish the payment versus a bank transfer, which is like what you use to pay your utility bills, you know, where you just like you give them like your routing number and your information and they pull a transfer from it each month.
That's a bank transfer.
And I would, we do that also.
So I wanted another blog post I'm just about as a bank transfer.
Okay, that works.
I'll take a look and see.
We might have that, but I'm not sure.
Yeah, you might already have it.
That's essentially what I'm requesting is two standalone posts.
One on like what is a bank redirect and one on what is a bank transfer.
Okay, okay.
That makes sense.
Yeah, the bank transfer one should be fairly straightforward.
Just have to make sure that it's B to C oriented, not B to C.
So, yeah, well, and it can be, I mean, like, it's B to B oriented meaning from us to them, not to a consumer, but the company that's reading it.
Could be using our bank transfer technology for an in-consumer to pay their utility bills or something.
Yeah, that makes sense.
Okay, that works.
So just the two ideas.
Okay, great.
those would just be helpful.
I've been trying to find my time to write those.
And I thought, like, I've got this team of people that can spit off blog posts.
I should just ask them to make these two.
Yeah, that is correct.
We actually have, for some of our clients, we create a separate tab in Airtable just for their content suggestions.
So it's just like a list.
One of our clients, he has, like, a bunch of newsletters he signed up for.
And then, like, when an interesting topic comes in, he sends it over and he's like, can we do something like this?
And a lot of times, like, the content that appears in newsletters, it doesn't always make for good SEO content, but we can, like, exact specific topics that we can cover.
So if you do have...
I'm sure most people do have, like, an inbox full of newsletters.
You can send some stuff over if you find something interesting.
Okay.
Let's make a tab like that.
That's a good idea.
Okay.
Yeah.
I'll add that in.
If already got that from our customers, add a suggestions tab.
We don't have to pull from it all the time, but anytime that something pops up, I can just put it in there and then we can talk about them.
Yeah.
Yeah.
That works.
Okay.
We'll do that.
These two, though, if you could just add to the list, they're not really.
I know they might not be the best for SEO, or we might already have them planned.
If we do, I'd like to prioritize them.
I just kind of need them for another use case, and it would be helpful to have growthx generate them.
Okay.
Okay.
That works.
Great.
What was the other thing you wanted to cover?
It was about topics and subtopics?
Topics and subtopics.
Yes.
I wasn't sure how we're handling this now.
I don't think we are.
And it creates a slight slowdown in our process.
It's okay the way it is, but let me share my screen.
I'll show you what.
So if we go to rapid.net, and I go to our blog, you can see any time that we publish a blog, we assign a topic to it from this list of topics, and based on the topic, we assign a subtopic.

It's a bit of a manual process now, because first I have to decide which topic it fits best in payments, then I have to click on that, I have to look at the subtopics, and decide which subtopic it fits best in.
And Tom does this for each of our blog posts.
It's not terrible to have things be manual, but in an attempt to try to automate things as much as possible, I'm wondering if there is a way to add the blog topic and subtopic to the...
creation process.
Yeah, we can do that.
I think we could do it, like, manually in Airtable.
Is that, like, helpful for Tom?
Does he know where it goes?
If we started manually in Airtable, that could work.
I'm kind of thinking we might be able to work it into the ingestion process to where it's part of, like, the metadata of the blog.
I think we might have something like that in your Aeroopsis, like, right now.
I think Kirkland may have built it in because there is, like, a topics column for when you're generating the XML file.
There's, like, a topic column.
And I think that's what Kirkland intended it for.
But I'll double check with him.
He's on vacation this week.
So when he comes back, I'll get an answer.
But it should be, like, pretty easy to do.
So, what are the topics?
Yeah, they're easy to find.
Okay.
I'll just walk you through the process.
I say they're easy to find.
But, you know, you can see all the topics kind of listed here or whatnot.
But the best way to find all of them is if you click on Blog in a Resource Center, you see the little line here that says See All Blogs?
Okay.
That drops you into a page where you have all the topics listed.
If it's grayed out, it means it's available.
It just means that we don't have any blogs on that topic.
So, for instance, like if I was to go in here and select Newsroom and look at our Press Releases 2, now you'd see like In the News is open.
So, all of these topics are available, even if they're grayed out.
It just means we haven't assigned anything to it at this point.
So, yeah, so this is our full list of topics.
Topics.
And then subtopics, the best way to get a full list of subtopics would actually be to select all of the topics.
Because then it's going to show every subtopic.
Okay.
Which is a lot.
We don't actually, I don't think you have to have a subtopic assigned to a blog.
So I'm wondering if maybe for ours, for like a manual process, it's pretty easy to like go, oh, it's a payments blog.
And then you just click on payments and see what subtopics are available.
But for an automated process, this might get complicated.
Let me see actually if I have this in a spreadsheet.
Hold on.
That might make this a lot easier if I've already done this.
Yeah, that makes sense.
Thank you.
Okay, topics.
Hey, look at that.
I got it right here.
Nice.
If I give you, let me see if this is what you need.
If I give you this.
Here's a little spreadsheet.
It's got topics and...
Subtopics.
Underneath that.
Yeah, does that give you, would that give you everything that you?
Yeah, this is like, this is pretty clear.
So we could do like a topic and a subtopic pretty easily.
I'll ask Kirkland if we can automate it.
But in the meantime, we can just like add these in manually.
Okay, let me, I'm just going to give this to you as a new document here.
Cool.
I'll stop sharing.
I'll just throw this in the slack when I'm done.
Okay, great.
Thank you.
Okay, so on the performance front.
So for last week, we saw, hold on, let show my screen.
So week 18, that was last week.
So organic traffic to our blogs almost doubled.
Which is nice to see.
Really?
Yeah.
Yeah.
it was just just the blogs, right?
Yeah.
Yeah.
So week 17 was 14 clicks.
Last week was 22.
And then we're making progress.
Yeah.
Yeah.
So we're making some nice progress here.
Really happy with like how we're progressing along.
think most of my clients get result like that, get results like this.
The one that didn't had really big problems with their website.
So usually if like your website is set up correctly, you don't have like a have like some major technical issues.
This is something that we should be seeing and it should continue to grow week over week.
Sometimes there are periods where like traffic kind of stagnates, especially if there's like a core update or something.
But this is looking pretty good three weeks into publishing.
I agree.
I
Okay, so this is traffic, and then what are our other metrics that we look at here?
Okay, so- URL, CTR, impressions, URL, click-through.
Yeah, so further down, this is just Google Search Console data, so this is looking at like the click-through rate, but if you go to the next page, this is where you see the GA4 data, so it's working.

The reason for the issue that we had previously was just the date range, so I had it set to automatic, and it wasn't working, so I had to like change it to like a manual one, so you can just, I think you can change the date range with permissions that you have already, but in this like graph, I mean chart, you can see like GA4 data, and this gives you like an insight into the bounce rate and the engagement rate.
The engagement rate is the opposite of the bounce rate, so an article that has a low bounce rate.
The high engagement rate and vice versa.
Okay.
So the like, I think GA4 counts an engagement if someone like scrolls on a page or if they click a link on a page.
So that counts as engagement or an engaged session.
Then.
If they scroll or click a link.
Okay.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
So one of those, one of the two or both.
And then further down, you see like these are the queries just from Google Search Console.
You're probably ranking for a lot more keywords, but Google Search Console doesn't provide all of the data that they might have.
So this is just like a snippet, I think, with search queries.
Are these, and obviously these aren't specific to the growthx blogs, it's just general search queries.
No, no, no, this is, this is specific to the growthx blogs.
Oh.
Yeah, yeah.
So this landing page filter right here, this is like a data control, so you've got to just filter to your URLs.
Okay.
Yeah.
And then the last slide we do, I think the LLM referral traffic is fixed.
We don't have that issue that we had previously.
Now we can kind of see a breakdown of the referral traffic to your site that comes from different LLMs.
Obviously, ChatGPT is like the biggest source of referral traffic in LLMs.
And to the right, you can see a table, which is basically just like, these are all of the pages, so it's not just growthx.
And it's like showing how many clicks it got, I think in the last, this is the last 28 days, I think.
Okay.
And this as well is the last 28 days.
Okay.
And this is showing which page they landed on, which LLM model provided that link, and how many views of that link.
Okay.
Yeah.
Yeah.
So it's pretty, pretty, pretty helpful.
You obviously are a much bigger website.
Some of my clients, it's just blog posts, but that's because they don't have, their website isn't a big to begin with.
So the blog is actually the biggest portion of their site.
That makes sense.
Mm-hmm.
Yeah.
I was going to ask you about, like, are you using SEMrush or Ahrefs internally?
I do use SEMrush internally.
Okay.

And if you have suggestions for how to configure reporting in SEMrush, how to configure, like, keyword lists in SEMrush, anything like that, if you want me to share anything with you in there, I'll...
I would love to go deep on that because I am really very much using it at a surface level right now for like, you know, keyword research and blog planning and stuff like that.
But I do not get as deep into the reporting as I think we could.
Yeah, so SEMrush has this thing called a position tracking report.
And I had a client add me to it.
So I just need to check if it was my email or if it was no, it was my email, definitely.
So they set up the position tracking report, and then they added me to it.
And then I could like add keywords to it.
And it's really helpful to just like track rankings over time and like visibility.
It was really helpful when like the last Google core update hit.
It was just immediate, like we could see like, what was the impact, like, over a couple of weeks.
And it's just a really nice way to like supplement the reporting that we already do.
Because sometimes, like some articles
Might lose traffic over time, and we're not sure if it was just, like, the article losing rankings or if it's, you know, other competitors, like, showing up in the SERP.
So, yeah, if you could, like, set up a position tracking report, that would be helpful.
How do I do this?
What do I need to do?
They have a new UI.
Yeah, I just logged in and saw that.
like, I love it when a tool that I've been using forever changes their UI and doesn't tell me that's great.
Yeah, yeah.
So that's actually, I think you should go to.
under reports.
No, I think it's SEO.
Ah.
Then position tracking.
Yeah.
Okay, so the ones that I've created in here, I have some that are organized by various different categories, and then some are made, like, this one here is just rando that can, I just need it.
I think it's, like, can make a new one.
Yeah.
A new one.
You could just call it like growthx keywords.
Okay.
It's going to be rapid.net obviously.
Yeah.
It's going to growthx keywords.
Okay.
Probably Google.
Hey, guess, chat GPT thing there.
Device, desktop, location.
Do I need to enter a location?
I think it's optional.
Yeah.
The problem is we do business in Europe, which is not a country, they don't let you do things regionally, so it's just not terribly helpful.
But I could go United Kingdom.
Yeah.
Yeah, let's do, I mean, we could do United Kingdom.
It's not that different.
That might screw things up a little bit, but who knows.
Okay.
Cool.
now.
Yeah, would have to add keywords in there.
Hold on, let me actually go back to the Airtable thing.

Okay, so you can just like copy and paste the keywords in this table just to start because these are the keywords that we are, we might not be targeting them, all of them right now, but we will at some point in time so we can just track these.
Okay.
All right, now I've got to figure out how to add you to this list.
Am going to make you a user on our account ready to share this with you?
Yeah, but… No.
No.
I'm sure how to do that.
I have to go my IT team to make you a user.
Maybe projects?
No.
Projects?
Yeah, there's like a projects tab when you go to users.
Oh, back to manage users?
Yeah, yeah.
Nice, okay.
If you click on growthx keywords, maybe you could add me there.
Let's just ask me to change the owner.
Maybe.
There we are.
This is what we wanted.
Growthx keywords.
We're going to...
We're
Find my email, because I have, like, I'm not sure which, okay, this is the one that I'm using.
Okay.
Oh, I see.
Anybody else?
Nicolette, I'm going add you here, too.
Is it in, uh, Nicolette, is it just Nicolette M at Rapyd.net?
Mm-hmm.
Did I spell that right?
N-I-C-O-L-E-T-T-E-M.
Yes, that's correct.
Okay.
Cool.
There.
Nice.
Okay, great.
I should get an email.
Email addresses.
You're, theoretically.
That's me, the shirt.
That emails again.
Okay, I have access to it.
That's great.
I'm going to edit the list of keywords in this project just because I know we're not targeting all of them, like some of the assignments you rejected.
So I'm just going to go through and make sure that we're actually like targeting the right keywords, just not to like dilute the data.
Okay, we've only got about 800 keywords left in our account that we can track.
To three up more, I need to delete some of those older projects, which I can do.
Okay, no problem.
I think we're not going to need like all 800 right now.
think we're pretty good.
Okay.
Okay, perfect.
I think that's about it.
Okay, we covered XML and you're going to throw that XML doc in the Slack and look for it there.
And then we talked about the top topics and subtopics.
I requested two new blog posts.
We looked at the position tracking report.
I just got to review more blogs, get them into you.
I don't have anything else.
If you don't, we can make this a very efficient meeting.
Yeah, sounds good.
All right.
Thank you.
I'll send you everything else in Slack.
Okay.
Nicolette, do have anything you need to add or any comments you want to share about your review process or anything before we go?
Nothing to note for my end.
All right.
Thanks a lot.
Thank you, guys.
See you next week.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Rapyd and GrowthX to discuss content performance, new content requests, and process improvements.](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=124.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Organic traffic to Rapyd blogs nearly doubled in the past week (14 clicks to 22)](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
  - [Two new blog post requests: "What is a bank redirect?" and "What is a bank transfer?"](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=151.0)
  - [SEMrush position tracking report set up for GrowthX keywords to monitor rankings](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=1219.0)
  - [XML file for first batch of articles ready; more to be added by end of week](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=191.0)

## Topics

### Content Performance Update

  - [Organic traffic to Rapyd blogs increased from 14 clicks (week 17) to 22 clicks (week 18)](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
  - [GA4 data now working correctly, showing bounce rate and engagement rate](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=986.0)
  - [LLM referral traffic issue resolved; breakdown of traffic from different LLMs available](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=1343.0)

### New Content Requests

  - [Two new blog posts requested by Mark:](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
    1.  "What is a bank redirect?"
    2.  "What is a bank transfer?"
  - [These posts will serve as reference articles for various payment methods Rapyd supports](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [A new "Suggestions" tab will be added to Airtable for future content ideas](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=445.0)

### Blog Topics and Subtopics

  - [Current manual process: Mark/Tom assign topic and subtopic when publishing](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=546.0)
  - [Aim to automate or streamline this process](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=584.0)
  - [Mark provided a spreadsheet with topics and subtopics for potential integration](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=668.0)

### SEMrush Position Tracking

  - [New position tracking report set up in SEMrush called "growthx keywords"](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=1219.0)
  - [Aida and Nicolette added as users to access the report](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=1501.0)
  - [Will help track rankings over time and monitor impact of Google updates](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=1219.0)

### XML File Update

  - [First batch of XML file ready with one article](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=197.0)
  - [Three additional reviewed articles to be added to the batch](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=207.0)
  - [Full batch expected to be sent by end of the week](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=210.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Aida to add "Suggestions" tab in Airtable for future content ideas](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=445.0)
  - [Kirkland to investigate automating topic/subtopic assignment in content creation process](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=627.0)
  - [Mark to share XML document and topics/subtopics spreadsheet in Slack](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=874.0)
  - [Aida to refine keyword list in SEMrush position tracking report](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=1385.0)
  - [Mark to review more blogs and move them to "Scheduled" status](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=249.0)
  - [Team to continue monitoring performance metrics and LLM referral traffic](https://fathom.video/share/Vyn1MVzDy5rLfSyV1Ye7L-ERvyy7EWDz?tab=summary&timestamp=1160.0)

action_items.description: Send XML document for first batch article in Slack
Add 2 new blog post requests to content list - "What is a bank redirect?" and "What is a bank transfer?"
Create new "Suggestions" tab in Airtable for Rapyd's content ideas
Manually add topics/subtopics to Airtable for blog posts (interim solution)
Ask Kirkland re: automating topic/subtopic assignment in XML generation process
Edit keyword list in new SEMrush position tracking project "growthx keywords"
